sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Bullishness Remains Steady as Geopolitical and Energy Concerns Rise, Morgan Stanley Wealth Management Pulse Survey Finds
feed.summary: A recent Morgan Stanley Wealth Management survey indicates that investor bullishness remains steady at 55%, despite a rise in concerns over geopolitical conflict and energy costs. A significant 63% of investors anticipate increased market volatility, with nearly half also worried about the impact of upcoming midterm elections on stock performance. Consequently, 50% of investors are dedicating more time to managing their portfolios, and they see opportunities primarily in information technology, energy, and healthcare sectors.

feed.summary: The Boeing Company (BA) reported Q1 revenue of $22.22 billion, surpassing the FactSet estimate of $21.85 billion. This earnings flash was published by MT Newswires, indicating a positive beat on revenue expectations for the aerospace giant.

feed.title: GE Aerospace Foundation Announces Inaugural "Lifting Futures" Workforce Locations
feed.summary: GE Aerospace has launched a $30 million "Lifting Futures" program to train 10,000 workers by 2030 in advanced manufacturing and aerospace roles. Five inaugural communities—Auburn, AL; Cincinnati-Dayton, OH; Dallas, TX; Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia; and Wrocław, Poland—have been selected to receive initial grants ranging from $500,000 to $1 million. The program aims to address the growing demand for skilled labor in the aerospace and manufacturing sectors by expanding training access and reducing barriers for students.

feed.title: HR Leaders Must Evolve Pay for Performance in the Age of AI
feed.summary: In an AI-enabled workplace, HR leaders must urgently adapt pay for performance strategies to address the growing impact of AI. This involves redefining an AI-ready performance philosophy, carefully applying AI for fast and fair performance assessment with guardrails, and differentiating pay using both human and machine intelligence. These changes are crucial because AI threatens the foundational elements of effective pay for performance by introducing new ambiguities, increasing bias risks, and complicating compensation decisions.

feed.title: New push aims to double annual volunteers to 150M by 2035
feed.summary: Points of Light, in partnership with Phillips 66, has launched the "Mobilizing Millions" campaign during Global Volunteer Month. This initiative aims to double the number of annual volunteers from 75 million to 150 million by 2035, aligning with America's 250th anniversary. The campaign seeks to address volunteer shortages and nonprofit funding challenges by strengthening the national volunteer infrastructure and inspiring more Americans to engage in service.

feed.title: DSV, United Airlines, Microsoft and Phillips 66 Ink Sustainable Aviation Fuel Deal
feed.summary: DSV, United Airlines, Microsoft, and Phillips 66 have partnered to provide large-scale access to sustainable aviation fuel (SAF), aiming to unlock up to 41.6 million liters of SAF and reduce lifecycle greenhouse gas emissions by approximately 100,000 tonnes. United Airlines will physically use the fuel, while DSV and Microsoft will participate through a book-and-claim model, allowing for transparent allocation of verified emissions reductions. This collaboration, the largest contracted SAF supply agreement with a single customer for United's Eco-Skies Alliance, utilizes ISCC certification and the SAFc Registry to ensure transparency and prevent double-counting of emissions reductions.

feed.title: McKesson Corporation Declares Quarterly Dividend
feed.summary: McKesson Corporation announced that its Board of Directors has declared a regular quarterly dividend of 82 cents per share of common stock. The dividend is scheduled to be paid on July 1, 2026, to shareholders of record as of June 1, 2026. McKesson, a diversified healthcare services leader, uses its investor relations website to post material information for shareholders.

feed.title: Ulta Beauty and Google Introduce Gemini-Enabled Shopping Experiences That Streamline Beauty Discovery and Purchase
feed.summary: Ulta Beauty and Google have partnered to launch new Gemini-enabled shopping experiences, including the integration of Ulta Beauty's product assortment into Google's AI Mode in Search and the Gemini app. Additionally, Ulta Beauty is introducing "Ulta AI," an AI shopping assistant built on Gemini Enterprise for Customer Experience, to provide personalized guidance on its own digital platforms. These initiatives aim to make beauty shopping more seamless, personalized, and intuitive for consumers.

feed.title: TE Connectivity sinks after mixed Q2 results (TEL:NYSE)

feed.title: After leaving data centers, AutoZone turns to Google AI tools
feed.summary: AutoZone has completed a three-year migration of its applications to Google Cloud, exiting legacy data centers. The company now plans to leverage Google AI Cloud and Gemini Enterprise to enhance system monitoring, accelerate development, and automate high-volume tasks. This strategic move aims to improve operational efficiency, decision-making speed, and overall productivity, building on its recent financial updates.

feed.title: Amid US ordeal, Moderna wins EU approval for flu/COVID-19 combo shot
feed.summary: Moderna has received European Commission approval for its combination flu and COVID-19 vaccine, mCOMBRIAX, for individuals aged 50 and above across EU member states, Iceland, Liechtenstein, and Norway. This approval marks a significant regulatory win for Moderna, especially given its ongoing challenges and uncertainties regarding the combo shot and its standalone flu vaccine (mRNA-1010) in the U.S. with the FDA. The European approval was granted despite mRNA-1010 not yet being approved as a standalone shot in the EU, a point of surprise for some analysts.

feed.title: Call Home Depot and an AI now answers in any language at U.S. stores
feed.summary: The Home Depot has launched AI-powered customer service phone agents, built on Google Cloud's Gemini Enterprise, for calls to its U.S. stores. A 50-store pilot demonstrated that these AI agents can understand caller intent in under 10 seconds and resolve issues four times faster than traditional phone menus, while also offering real-time language translation. The company plans to expand this system to all U.S. stores over the next year, aiming to improve customer experience and free up associates for complex tasks.

feed.title: CHIPOTLE REPORTS FIRST CUMULATIVE REAL FOODPRINT RESULTS, INCLUDING OVER 616 MILLION GALLONS OF WATER SAVED IN 2025
feed.summary: Chipotle Mexican Grill announced its first cumulative Real Foodprint results, revealing that its sustainable sourcing practices in 2025 helped avoid over 10,060 metric tons of carbon emissions and saved more than 616 million gallons of water. This initiative, part of its 2025 Sustainability Report Update, highlights the environmental benefits of their ingredient sourcing standards compared to conventional practices. The report also details progress across its People, Food & Animals, and Environment pillars, and sets new sustainability goals for 2030, such as increasing operational diversion rates and supporting agricultural practices that promote soil and water well-being.

feed.title: PNB MetLife Launches RISE Plan to Support Long-Term Income Planning and Life Protection
feed.summary: PNB MetLife has launched its new RISE Plan, an individual, non-linked, non-participating savings life insurance product designed to offer guaranteed income and comprehensive life cover. The plan caters to growing demand for stable income solutions and financial preparedness for life milestones. It includes flexible options like Smart Income and Smart Income Pro, providing customers choices in income commencement and payout methods, alongside tax benefits and optional riders.

feed.title: Corie Barry stepping down as Best Buy CEO; Jason Bonfig named successor
feed.summary: Corie Barry will step down as CEO of Best Buy Co. Inc. on October 31, 2026, after leading the electronics retailer through significant market changes and expansions. Jason Bonfig has been named as her successor. Barry navigated the company through the pandemic and into new ventures like furniture and healthcare.

feed.title: Back to the Roots Scales Regenerative Movement Nationwide; Expanding Pioneering Soil Health Set to Walmart Stores for Year Two
feed.summary: Back to the Roots, a leading organic gardening company, is expanding its Soil Health Set to Walmart stores nationwide for a second year. This 12-product collection aims to educate gardeners on treating soil as a living ecosystem rather than just "dirt," promoting regenerative practices. The initiative focuses on providing holistic tools, including organic worm castings, trace minerals, and cover crops, to enhance soil structure, mineral density, and biological activity, aligning with a growing market for "Eco-Optimist" products.

feed.title: JPMorgan and Morgan Stanley Bullish on Western Digital (WDC)
feed.summary: JPMorgan and Morgan Stanley have expressed bullish sentiments on Western Digital (WDC), raising their price targets due to anticipated strong growth in AI-related suppliers and increased HDD demand. JPMorgan analyst Samik Chatterjee increased the price target to $400, citing AI infrastructure investments, while Morgan Stanley raised its target to $380 based on HDD demand. Despite acknowledging the potential of WDC, Insider Monkey suggests that other AI stocks may offer higher returns in a shorter timeframe.
